# AI Development Workflow

> A spec-first workflow for AI-assisted development at companies shipping to real users. Built around one principle: precise specs make AI-written code reviewable and shippable at the same bar as human-written code. Extracted from a real case study where the workflow ran to its extreme — 1 PM + AI built a microservice in 38 days for ~10× less core-team capacity than the traditional estimate — but applies the same way to developers using AI as a pair-programmer.

## Workflow

- [Workflow — the 11 stages](./workflow.md): The full 11-stage pipeline. Pre-Dev validates the spec and architecture before coding. Post-Dev fixes code via reviewers themselves.
- [Spec & prototype requirements](./spec-requirements.md): Quality bar for the spec and the prototype. Required sections, principles, anti-patterns.
- [When to use this workflow](./when-to-use.md): Honest fit assessment. Who this is built for, what modes it supports, when it doesn't fit.
- [Case study](./case-study.md): The extreme example — 1 PM + AI, 38 days. Numbers, timeline, what worked, what got stuck, what scales beyond this case.

## Templates

- [Spec template](./templates/spec-template.md): Drop-in spec template with all required sections.
- [Prototype checklist](./templates/prototype-checklist.md): Checklist for prototype handoff to design and dev.
- [Pre-Dev QA checklist](./templates/pre-dev-qa-checklist.md): Gate checklist before development starts.

## Meta

- [README](./README.md): Repository entry point and TL;DR.
- [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md): Reading order and usage notes for Claude Code.
